honoree.name: Governor Thomas Kirker
honoree.summary: Thomas Kirker was an American politician who served as the second Governor of Ohio from 1807 to 1808. Born in County Down, Ireland, he immigrated to the United States and settled in Ohio. He was a member of the Ohio House of Representatives and also served as a justice of the Ohio Supreme Court. Kirker was a significant figure in early Ohio state politics.

honoree.name: Corporal John E. Ruch
honoree.summary: Corporal John Ruch was killed in an automobile accident during a high-speed pursuit on US Route 20 in Ashtabula County. He and another patrolman were en route to a defense meeting when they attempted to stop a speeding vehicle. Their patrol car went out of control on ice and snow and struck two trees. Corporal Ruch had served with the agency for 8 years and was stationed in Geneva at the time. He was survived by his wife and parents.

honoree.name: Duncan McArthur
honoree.summary: Duncan McArthur was born on December 14, 1772, in Dutchess County, New York, and died on April 28, 1839, in Chillicothe, Ohio. He was an American politician and military officer who served as the 11th Governor of Ohio. McArthur represented Ohio in the U.S. House of Representatives and served as a brigadier general in the War of 1812. His career was significant in the early political and military development of the state of Ohio.

honoree.name: Governor Robert Lucas
honoree.summary: Robert Lucas was born on April 4, 1781, in Shepherdstown, Virginia, and died on February 7, 1853, in Iowa City, Iowa. He served as the 12th governor of Ohio from 1832 to 1836 and later as the first territorial governor of Iowa from 1838 to 1841. Lucas was a prominent political figure known for his leadership during the state's early development and his role in the Toledo War. His legacy includes significant contributions to the governance and expansion of the Midwestern United States.

honoree.name: William Green
honoree.summary: William Oxley Thompson was an American academic administrator born on January 5, 1855, in Cambria, Wisconsin. He served as the president of The Ohio State University from 1926 to 1938, significantly expanding the university's campus and academic programs. Thompson was a Presbyterian minister and educator who held several leadership positions in higher education throughout his career. He died on December 13, 1935, in Columbus, Ohio, leaving a legacy of institutional growth and educational reform.
honoree.education: Miami University, Bachelor of Arts, Lane Theological Seminary, Bachelor of Divinity, University of Chicago, Doctor of Divinity

Carl B. Stokes
honoree.summary: Carl Stokes was an American politician and diplomat born on June 21, 1927, in Cleveland, Ohio. He served as the mayor of Cleveland from 1968 to 1971, becoming the first African American mayor of a major U.S. city. After his mayoral term, he worked as a television news anchor and later served as the U.S. Ambassador to the Seychelles. His career was marked by pioneering achievements in civil rights and urban governance.

honoree.name: Branch Rickey
honoree.summary: Branch Rickey was an American baseball player and executive born on December 20, 1881. He is most famous for breaking Major League Baseball's color barrier by signing Jackie Robinson to the Brooklyn Dodgers in 1947. His career also included roles as a manager, coach, and innovator who developed the farm system. He died on December 9, 1965, leaving a lasting legacy in sports history.

honoree.name: Thaddeus Kosciusko
honoree.summary: Tadeusz Kościuszko was a Polish-Lithuanian military engineer and statesman born on February 4, 1746, who became a hero of the American Revolutionary War. He served as a colonel in the Continental Army, designing key fortifications such as West Point. Kościuszko later led the 1794 Kościuszko Uprising against Russian and Prussian rule in Poland. He is remembered as a national hero in both the United States and Poland for his fight for freedom and independence.
honoree.education: Royal School of Artillery and Engineering in Warsaw, École Militaire in Paris

honoree.name: Patrolman Paul L. McManis
honoree.summary: Patrolman Paul McManis was killed in an automobile crash on State Route 269, five miles north of Bellevue while responding to another crash. The patrol car was clipped while passing another vehicle which did not signal that they were making a left turn. The patrol car spun around, and both officers were thrown from the car. Patrolman McManis was killed instantly. The other officer suffered serious injuries. Patrolman McManis had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for five months and was stationed at Bellevue.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Automobile crash

highways.name: U.S. Navy Corpsman Maxton Soviak Memorial Highway
Patrolman Leroy S. Bedell Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Patrolman Leroy S. Bedell
honoree.summary: Patrolman LeRoy Bedell was killed in a motorcycle crash while patrolling State Route 59 in Erie County, six miles east of Bellevue, at 3:35 pm. A dog lunged at his motorcycle, causing him to lose control as he attempted to avoid striking it. He was thrown onto the highway and suffered a broken neck. He was transported to Bellevue Hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries 15 minutes later. Patrolman Bedell had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for two years, was assigned to Bellevue, and previously served with the Heron County Sheriff's Office. He is survived by his wife, five children, and mother. State Route 59 is current day OH 113.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Motorcycle crash

highways.name: Patrolman Jerry R. Neff Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Patrolman Jerry R. Neff
honoree.summary: Patrolman Jerry Neff was killed when a suspect driving a stolen vehicle recklessly drove off of a highway overpass and crashed into Patrolman Neff's patrol car below. The suspect's car landed on top of the patrol car at the junction of I-71 and US Route 62 at 9:15 a.m. The 19-year-old driver was charged with vehicular homicide and reckless driving. His 20-year-old passenger was charged with vehicular homicide and motor vehicle theft. Patrolman Neff had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for three years and was assigned to the Circleville Post at the time of the incident. He was survived by his wife, three children, and father.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Vehicular assault Weapon: Automobile Offender: Charged with vehicular homicide

highways.name: Trooper Jody S. Dye Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Trooper Jody S. Dye
honoree.summary: Trooper Jody Dye was struck and killed while placing flares in the roadway at a crash scene at the interchange of I-270 and US Route 33 in Columbus at 10 pm. He was transported to a Columbus hospital, where he succumbed to his injuries an hour later. Trooper Dye had served with the Ohio Highway Patrol for over three years and was assigned to the Granville Post. He is survived by his wife and two young daughters.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Struck by vehicle

honoree.name: Martin Luther King, Jr
honoree.summary: Martin Luther King Jr. was born on January 15, 1929, in Atlanta, Georgia, and became a Baptist minister and civil rights leader. He is renowned for his advocacy of nonviolent resistance and his leadership in the American Civil Rights Movement. King delivered his iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech during the 1963 March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom. He was assassinated on April 4, 1968, in Memphis, Tennessee, and is remembered as a pivotal figure in the fight for racial equality.
honoree.education: Morehouse College, Crozer Theological Seminary, Boston University

highways.name: Officer Sonny L. Kim Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Officer Sonny L. Kim
honoree.summary: Police Officer Sonny Kim was shot and killed after responding to a 911 call at a home near the intersection of Roe Street, and Whetsel Avenue at approximately 9:30 am. The caller stated a man was walking on the sidewalk at that location with a gun in his waistband and acting belligerent. Officer Kim was the first officer to arrive at the location and was approached by the subject, who made the 911 call. The man immediately opened fire on Officer Kim, wounding him. He then struggled with Officer Kim and disarmed him. The man then used Officer Kim's gun to shoot at a Hamilton County probation officer and a second Cincinnati police officer who responded to the scene. The second officer returned fire, fatally wounding the subject. Responding officers performed CPR on Officer Kim until medics arrived and transported him to the hospital. He succumbed to his wounds a short time later. It was later determined that the subject had intended to commit suicide by cop. Officer Kim had served with the Cincinnati Police Department for 27 years. He is survived by his wife and three sons. In February 2020, East Hyde Park Commons was renamed Officer Sonny Kim Park in memory of Officer Kim.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Weapon: Handgun Offender: Shot and killed

highways.name: Spc. Donald S. "Scott" Morrison Memorial Highway
Lance Corporal Christopher J. Dyer USMC, Lima Co, 3/25 Marines Memorial Highway
Officers Dennis Bennington and Robert Seiffert Memorial Highway
Patrolman David L. Cole Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Patrolman David L. Cole
honoree.summary: Patrolman David Cole was shot and killed after responding to an alarm call at United Dairy Farmers at 2373 Florence Avenue. When he arrived on the scene, he observed two men walking nearby. As he approached them, they opened fire, wounding him twice. One of the suspects then took Patrolman Cole's revolver and shot him again. Patrolman Cole was transported to a local hospital where he succumbed to his wounds. Both suspects, both 23, were sentenced to death but had the sentences commuted to life. One of the men was paroled in 1991 and moved to California. The other suspect has been denied parole four times, and the next hearing is on March 1, 2027. Patrolman Cole was an Ohio National Guard veteran and had served with the Cincinnati Police Department for five years. His father was also a Cincinnati Police Officer. He was survived by his wife, parents, and four siblings. In September 2021, a street near Florence and Gilbert Avenues in Walnut Hills was named after Patrolman David Cole.  In 2023, a portion of Interstate 71 was dedicated as “Patrolman David L. Cole Memorial Highway.” He is buried in Saint Peter Cemetery, New Richmond, Ohio.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Incident Date: Sunday, July 14, 1974 Weapon: Officer's handgun Offender: Two sentenced to life. One paroled

honoree.name: Johnny Appleseed
honoree.summary: Johnny Appleseed, born John Chapman, was an American pioneer and nurseryman who introduced apple trees to large parts of the United States. He was born in Massachusetts in 1774 and died in Fort Wayne, Indiana, in 1845. His work helped establish apple orchards and provided a source of food and cider for settlers. He is remembered as a legendary figure and a symbol of American frontier spirit.

highways.name: Congressman Michael G. Oxley Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Congressman Michael G. Oxley
honoree.summary: Michael Oxley was an American politician who served as a U.S. Representative from Ohio. He was born on February 11, 1944, in Findlay, Ohio, and died on January 1, 2016. Oxley was a Republican member of Congress, best known for co-sponsoring the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002, which reformed corporate governance and accounting. His legislative career significantly impacted financial regulation and corporate accountability in the United States.
honoree.education: Miami University, Ohio State University, Moritz College of Law

honoree.name: Patrolman Frank J. Hossler
honoree.summary: Patrolman Frank Hossler was killed in an automobile crash while on patrol at 7:30 p.m. Patrolman Hossler was traveling north on State Route 69 (present-day SR 235) when his cruiser collided with another vehicle. He died at the scene. Patrolman Hossler was a United States Marine Corps Korean War Purple Heart recipient and had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for 10 months, assigned to the Bellefontaine Post. He was survived by his expectant wife.

honoree.name: Rosa Parks
honoree.summary: Rosa Parks was an American activist born on February 4, 1913, in Tuskegee, Alabama, who became a pivotal figure in the Civil Rights Movement. She is best known for her refusal to give up her seat on a Montgomery bus, which sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott. Her actions challenged racial segregation and inspired widespread activism for civil rights. She died on October 24, 2005, in Detroit, Michigan.

honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Weapon: Handgun Offender: Committed suicide

honoree.name: Officer Brandon Stalker
honoree.summary: Police Officer Brandon Stalker was shot and killed at about 6:30 pm during a barricade involving an arson suspect. At approximately 2:20 am, the front doors of the historic Rosary Cathedral had been set on fire, and racial statements were spray painted onto the walls. At approximately 3:30 pm, officers spotted the suspect near his house in the 2300 block of Fulton Street and attempted to talk to him. The man drew a handgun and ran into a home where he barricaded himself inside. The department's SWAT team responded to the scene and, after negotiations failed, fired a chemical irritant into the home in an attempt to force him outside. The man emerged from the home holding two handguns and opened fire. Officer Stalker, who was on a perimeter position, was struck in the head and fatally wounded. Other officers returned fire and killed the subject. Officer Stalker had served with the Toledo Police Department for 2-1/2 years. He is survived by his two children and fiancée.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Weapon: Handgun Offender: Shot and killed

honoree.name: Patrolman William J. Keller
honoree.summary: Patrolman William Keller was struck and killed by a drunk driver while conducting a traffic stop on I-76 in Portage County. At 9:00 p.m., Patrolman Keller stopped a driver a mile west of the Ravenna exit (current day Prospect Street) when a vehicle traveling east hit the car, knocking Patrolman Keller into the roadway. The driver fled the scene. The driver was apprehended but died before standing trial. Patrolman Keller was a United States Army Vietnam War veteran and had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for 10 months, assigned to the Ravenna Post. He was survived by his wife, child, and parents.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Vehicular assault Weapon: Automobile; Alcohol involved Offender: Not available

honoree.name: Duncan McArthur
honoree.summary: Duncan McArthur was born on December 14, 1772, in Dutchess County, New York, and died on April 28, 1839, in Chillicothe, Ohio. He was an American politician and military officer who served as the 11th Governor of Ohio. McArthur represented Ohio in the U.S. House of Representatives and served as a brigadier general in the War of 1812. His career was significant in the early political and military development of the state of Ohio.
honoree.placeOfBirth: Dutchess County, New York, United States

honoree.name: Branch Rickey
honoree.summary: Branch Rickey was an American baseball player and executive born on December 20, 1881. He is most famous for breaking Major League Baseball's color barrier by signing Jackie Robinson to the Brooklyn Dodgers in 1947. His career also included roles as a manager, coach, and innovator who developed the farm system. He died on December 9, 1965, leaving a lasting legacy in sports history.
honoree.education: Ohio Wesleyan University, University of Michigan Law School

honoree.name: Patrolman James P. Gardner
honoree.summary: Patrolman James Gardner was killed in an automobile crash while pursuing a vehicle at 8:20 p.m. His patrol car collided with another vehicle and then struck a telephone pole on Highway 62 near Alliance. The other patrolman in the vehicle was seriously injured. Patrolman Gardner was a U.S. Navy veteran of WWII and had served with the Ohio State Highway Patrol for only 5 months, assigned to the Massillon Post. He was survived by his parents.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Automobile crash

honoree.name: Patrolman Sean VanDenberg
honoree.summary: Patrolman Sean VanDenberg died from complications as a result of contracting COVID-19 from arresting and transporting a COVID-19 positive suspect to jail after serving a warrant at a home in the 15400 block of Wooster Street NW in North Lawrence. Patrolman VanDenberg had served with the Lawrence Township Police Department for eight years. He is survived by his wife, four children, and two grandchildren.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: COVID19 Incident Date: Friday, December 3, 2021

honoree.name: Marine Corps League
honoree.summary: James David Vance is an American politician and author born in Middletown, Ohio. He served as a U.S. Senator from Ohio from 2023 to 2025 and is the Republican nominee for Vice President in the 2024 election. Vance is best known for his memoir 'Hillbilly Elegy,' which explores cultural and economic issues in Appalachia. He previously served in the United States Marine Corps and worked in the technology and venture capital industries.
honoree.education: Ohio State University, Yale Law School

honoree.name: William Green
honoree.summary: William Oxley Thompson was an American academic administrator born on January 5, 1855, in Cambria, Wisconsin. He served as the president of The Ohio State University from 1926 to 1938, significantly expanding the university's campus and academic programs. Thompson was a Presbyterian minister and educator who held several leadership positions in higher education throughout his career. He died on December 13, 1935, in Columbus, Ohio, leaving a legacy of institutional growth and educational reform.
honoree.education: Miami University, Bachelor of Arts, Lane Theological Seminary, Bachelor of Divinity, University of Chicago, Doctor of Divinity

honoree.name: Johnny Appleseed
honoree.summary: Johnny Appleseed, born John Chapman, was an American pioneer and nurseryman who introduced apple trees to large parts of the United States. He was born in Massachusetts in 1774 and died in Fort Wayne, Indiana, in 1845. His work helped establish apple orchards and provided a source of food and cider for settlers. He is remembered as a legendary figure and a symbol of American frontier spirit.

honoree.name: Governor Robert Lucas
honoree.summary: Robert Lucas was born on April 4, 1781, in Shepherdstown, Virginia, and died on February 7, 1853, in Iowa City, Iowa. He served as the 12th governor of Ohio from 1832 to 1836 and later as the first territorial governor of Iowa from 1838 to 1841. Lucas was a prominent political figure known for his leadership during the state's early development and his role in the Toledo War. His legacy includes significant contributions to the governance and expansion of the Midwestern United States.

honoree.name: Duncan McArthur
honoree.summary: Duncan McArthur was born on December 14, 1772, in Dutchess County, New York, and died on April 28, 1839, in Chillicothe, Ohio. He was an American politician and military officer who served as the 11th Governor of Ohio. McArthur represented Ohio in the U.S. House of Representatives and served as a brigadier general in the War of 1812. His career was significant in the early political and military development of the state of Ohio.
honoree.placeOfBirth: Dutchess County, New York, United States

highways.name: Natural Resources Officer Jason Lagore Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Natural Resources Officer Jason Lagore
honoree.summary: Natural Resources Officer Jason Lagore died after suffering a medical emergency while conducting a search for a juvenile who had fallen through the ice at Rocky Fork Lake at Rocky Fork State Park. Two juveniles had fallen through the ice but one was able to get to shore. Officer Lagore and other first responders were searching for the other juvenile when he suffered a medical emergency. He was transported Highland District Hospital where he was pronounced dead. The second juvenile was recovered from the water a short time later and also pronounced dead at a local hospital. Officer Lagore had served with the Ohio Department of Natural Resources - Division of State Parks and Watercraft for 15 years. He is survived by his wife and two young sons.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Duty related illness

honoree.name: State Representative Andy Thompson
honoree.summary: Andy Thompson is an American politician from Ohio who served as a member of the Ohio House of Representatives. He represented the 95th district, which covers Carroll, Harrison, and Jefferson counties, among others. His political career was marked by his focus on fiscal responsibility and local governance issues. He is known for his contributions to state legislation during his tenure in the Ohio General Assembly.

He is remembered as a legendary figure and a symbol of American frontier spirit.

honoree.name: Rosa Parks
honoree.summary: Rosa Parks was an American activist born on February 4, 1913, in Tuskegee, Alabama, who became a pivotal figure in the Civil Rights Movement. She is best known for her refusal to give up her seat on a Montgomery bus, which sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott. Her actions challenged racial segregation and inspired widespread activism for civil rights. She died on October 24, 2005, in Detroit, Michigan.
